title: GetResponse vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare GetResponse vs Constant Contact. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool wins for your business needs.
h1: GetResponse vs Constant Contact: Complete Comparison
intro: GetResponse and Constant Contact are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. GetResponse excels for creators and course sellers with webinar and funnel-building tools, while Constant Contact focuses on simplicity for local businesses and nonprofits. This guide breaks down their key differences to help you choose the right platform.

comparison_table.tool_b: SMS, social media ads, e-commerce, event management

choose_a_if: You want to start for free with up to 500 subscribers
You host webinars or create and sell online courses
You need advanced automation and conversion funnels
You want higher feature depth at a lower entry cost
choose_b_if: You need SMS marketing and social media ad integration
You run an e-commerce store or manage events
You prefer a simpler, more streamlined interface
You want straightforward pricing with no free tier complications

faq.q: Is GetResponse better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: It depends on your use case. GetResponse is better for content creators, course sellers, and webinar hosts who need advanced automation and funnel-building. Constant Contact is better for local businesses and nonprofits that prioritize simplicity, SMS marketing, and e-commerce features. Both are solid platforms with comparable ratings.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, GetResponse or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Constant Contact starts at $12/month, while GetResponse starts at $19/month for paid plans. However, GetResponse offers a free tier supporting 500 subscribers with full feature access, making it the better value if you're starting out. Constant Contact has no free option but may be cheaper for very small lists.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to GetResponse?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support email list imports and exports. Most email marketing tools allow you to export your contacts as a CSV file and import them into GetResponse. The transition is straightforward, though you may need to rebuild email sequences and automation workflows in the new platform.
